title: Custom Domains DNS Ownership TLS Canonical Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the custom domains dns ownership tls canonical capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: A custom domain progresses through an explicit lifecycle: creating an ownership challenge yields a pending-DNS state, verifying the expected DNS TXT token moves it to verified, activating the auth client moves it to active, and proving local routing moves it to a routing-locally-active state. From there a domain can be set or unset as canonical, deactivated (which clears auth-client, canonical, and routing state), or disabled (which frees the enabled-domain claim while retaining history).

stateModel.description: The registry record tracks ownership status (pending-DNS, DNS-mismatch, verified), auth-client status (inactive, active), routing status (unknown, locally-active), a TLS status held at unknown for local proofs, a canonical flag, a redirect policy (no-redirect or redirect-slug-to-canonical), lifecycle timestamps, a disabled marker, and associated challenge token rows. Public DNS and TLS readiness are represented as deferred rather than asserted by local state.

permissions.description: All domain operations are tenant-scoped through domain read/write permissions, and auth-client mutation is performed via an injected auth-client-domain port only after ownership has been verified. Cross-tenant enabled-domain claims are rejected so one tenant cannot claim a domain already enabled for another, preserving tenant isolation over the global domain namespace.

contracts.description: The interface covers challenge creation, DNS verification, auth-client activation and deactivation, a local routing probe, canonical set and unset, and a list/readiness projection, all behind a tenant-domain port with auth-client and DNS-challenge adapters. Operation semantics are expressed against the port so the registry contract is independent of the specific identity-provider or DNS technology.

validation.description: Validation rejects malformed domain names and IP literals, requires the expected verification TXT token before a domain can be verified, and refuses canonical designation until the domain is verified, auth-client active, and locally routed. Cross-tenant claims on an already-enabled domain are rejected before any challenge token is issued.

errorModel.description: Outcomes are typed and fail-closed, including invalid-domain, domain-already-claimed, not-found, expired, already-verified, DNS-not-found, DNS-mismatch, not-verified, auth-client-inactive, routing-not-proven, and not-active alongside the success case. Conflict and failure paths never leak the DNS verification token, so error responses cannot expose ownership secrets.

auditModel.description: Mutations emit safe, metadata-only audit events for challenge creation, conflict rejection, verification, auth-client activation and deactivation, local routing proof, and canonical set and unset. Conflict paths in particular emit audit without ever including the DNS token, and audit precedes the corresponding state change.

readinessModel.description: Readiness distinguishes no-domains, pending-DNS, verified, inactive auth-client, locally-routed, deferred public DNS/TLS, and canonical redirect cutover states. Local proofs explicitly decline to claim public DNS or TLS readiness, so the capability degrades honestly to a local-only posture when external infrastructure is absent.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by runtime proofs covering domain challenge and verification, local routing, canonical cutover, claim lifecycle, custom-domain resolution, and custom-domain auth origin against a live substrate with injected ports. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
